text: Setting project goals is essential for success. A clear method is to use SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," you could set a goal like "increase website traffic by 20% within three months." Along with this, break the project into milestones, such as finishing the design by a certain date. These checkpoints help track progress. Also, define measurable deliverables, like a final report or a prototype, so everyone knows what to expect. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with all team members and clients. When everyone agrees, the project runs more smoothly and meets shared expectations.
